name: Tender Letter
summary: Drafts a comprehensive formal tender letter serving as official notice of payment or performance of contractual obligations. Used in personal injury litigation to fulfill obligations precisely while preserving the sender's legal rights and defenses. Structures the letter with detailed reviews of contracts, invoices, and correspondence for evidentiary grounding.
content:
You are tasked with drafting a comprehensive formal tender letter that serves as an official legal notice of payment or performance of contractual obligations. This letter must be precise, legally sound, and professionally structured to protect the sender's legal position while clearly communicating their intent to fulfill their obligations under the governing agreement.

Before beginning the drafting process, conduct a thorough review of all relevant documents in the matter file to extract specific factual details that will ground this tender letter in concrete evidence. Search for and identify the underlying contract or agreement, including its execution date, parties, contract number, and specific provisions governing payment or performance obligations. Locate any invoices, payment schedules, purchase orders, or statements of work that define the amount owed and the terms of tender. Review all prior correspondence between the parties to understand the chronology of the relationship, including any payment demands, notices of default, cure period notifications, or attempts at resolution. Extract specific dates, amounts, account numbers, wire transfer instructions, and any other logistical details that will be necessary to effectuate the tender. Identify the precise legal names and capacities of both the sender and recipient, along with their complete contact information and any authorized representatives involved in the transaction.

Structure the tender letter to begin with a comprehensive introduction that establishes the sender's complete legal identity, including their full name, capacity (whether individual, corporate entity, authorized representative, or legal agent), and all relevant contact information including physical address, email, and telephone number. Identify the recipient with equal specificity and establish the foundational relationship by referencing the underlying agreement with precise identifying details such as contract numbers, dates of execution, and parties involved. This opening section should eliminate any ambiguity about which obligation is being addressed and create an unassailable record of the parties' identities and relationship.

Articulate the precise purpose of this communication by explicitly stating that this constitutes a formal legal tender under applicable law. Specify with exactitude the amount being tendered, the currency, and the specific obligation it satisfies, referencing invoice numbers, payment schedules, or contractual milestones as documented in the source materials. Detail the proposed method of tender, whether by wire transfer, certified bank draft, cashier's check, or other commercially acceptable means, and confirm the sender's unconditional readiness to perform all obligations in accordance with the terms of the underlying agreement. Include language making clear that this tender is made without prejudice to any rights or claims the sender may have, preserving all defenses and remedies that may exist independently of this performance.

Provide sufficient factual and chronological context by narrating the relevant history of the commercial or contractual relationship as evidenced by the documents in the file. Trace the evolution of the obligation from its inception through any material developments, including prior payment demands, correspondence exchanged between the parties, notices of default or breach, cure periods that have elapsed, and any attempts at resolution or negotiation. This narrative should demonstrate good faith and establish a clear record of the sender's compliance efforts while documenting any failures or delays attributable to the recipient. Use specific dates, quote relevant passages from correspondence, and cite to particular documents to create a factual foundation that can withstand scrutiny in potential litigation.

Ground the tender in its proper legal foundation by identifying the specific contractual provisions, statutory requirements, or common law principles that govern the obligation and authorize this method of performance. Reference the actual contract language by section number and quote pertinent provisions where doing so strengthens the legal position. Assert clearly that this tender constitutes complete, timely, and lawful fulfillment of all obligations owed by the sender under the referenced agreement, and that acceptance of this tender will satisfy and discharge the sender's liability in full. If there are any legal authorities that support the validity and sufficiency of this tender under the circumstances, incorporate appropriate citations to strengthen the legal foundation.

Deliver formal notice to the recipient that refusal or failure to accept this valid tender may result in significant legal consequences. Specify that wrongful rejection may lead to the cessation of interest accrual from the date of tender, the shifting of costs and attorney's fees to the recipient, the loss of remedies for non-performance, potential liability for damages arising from wrongful rejection, and the establishment of the sender's defense to any claims of breach or default. Expressly reserve all of the sender's rights, remedies, claims, and defenses under the contract, at law, and in equity, including the right to seek declaratory relief confirming the sufficiency of the tender, damages for breach, reimbursement of costs incurred, and any other relief deemed appropriate should the recipient fail to acknowledge or accept this tender in accordance with its terms.

Conclude with clear, actionable instructions for acceptance that specify exactly how, where, and when the tendered payment or performance will be delivered upon the recipient's acknowledgment. Provide the relevant bank account details, delivery address, or other logistical information necessary to effectuate the transfer, drawing these details from the contract or prior correspondence as appropriate. Establish a reasonable but firm deadline by which the recipient must acknowledge receipt of this letter and confirm their acceptance of the tender, typically between seven and fourteen business days from the date of the letter. Close with language that is professional and courteous yet unmistakably firm in conveying the seriousness of this legal communication and the sender's expectation of prompt compliance.

Format the signature block to include the full legal name of the signatory, their official title or capacity, the name of the entity they represent if applicable, and the date of execution. Ensure the letter references appropriate letterhead if representing a company or organization, and consider whether the circumstances warrant notarization or witness signatures to enhance the evidentiary weight of the document based on the amount at stake and the relationship between the parties.

Throughout the letter, maintain a tone that is formal, professional, and legally authoritative without being unnecessarily adversarial. The goal is to demonstrate good faith performance while simultaneously creating a protective legal record. Use precise legal terminology where appropriate but ensure the language remains accessible and unambiguous to facilitate understanding and acceptance. Structure the narrative to flow logically from identification through context, legal basis, notice, and conclusion, creating a cohesive document that serves both as a good-faith attempt to perform obligations and as a protective legal record should disputes arise.

The final tender letter should be comprehensive enough to stand alone as a complete legal document, supported by specific facts drawn from the matter file, grounded in applicable legal principles, and structured to maximize both the likelihood of acceptance and the strength of the sender's legal position in the event of rejection or dispute.
